data.body:
CONVERSION/ROUND-TRIP RESEARCH (wf_c717e2e3-917; 104 agents, 25/25 confirmed). Closes the visual-layer loop.

HEADLINE: there is NO zero-bespoke off-the-shelf solution for {persisted custom IR + ephemeral
off-the-shelf visual editor + reliable round-trip}; SOME thin mapping is unavoidable. The smallest proven
path = a THIN, ID-preserving IR<->standard-format SERIALIZER feeding an off-the-shelf EMBEDDABLE editor
that reads that standard NATIVELY as a transient projection. The serializer is the ONLY bespoke piece.

LLM-EDIT SIDE (Thread A) — converged 2025-2026 pattern: route LLM edits through a concise, ordered,
schema-constrained JSON IR via ATOMIC, ID-KEYED operations (add/delete/move/update/redirect), NOT raw-XML
and NOT whole-document rewrite. Evidence: BPMN Assistant arXiv 2509.24592 (peer-pub MDPI App.Sci.):
JSON-IR+atomic-ops cut editing FAILURES 11->2, output tokens >75%, latency ~43% across GPT-5.1/Claude-4.5-
Sonnet/DeepSeek-V3. NUANCE: the XML penalty is concentrated in EDITING; de-novo GENERATION handles XML
fine. Grammar/structured-output decoding (llguidance — Python bindings; Outlines/Guidance/XGrammar) can
guarantee syntactic validity; for hosted Claude, rely on provider tool-use/JSON-schema.

VISUAL ROUND-TRIP SIDE (Threads B/C):
- AVOID the MDE frameworks (Eclipse GLSP / Sirius Web / Sprotty / Langium): all are frameworks-you-build-on
  (need a custom metamodel->graphical mapping = the bespoke layer we want to avoid) AND runtime-locked —
  GLSP/Sprotty/Langium JS/browser-locked, Sirius Web JVM-locked + needs PostgreSQL + persists the model in
  a DB (violates "nothing persisted"). Hostile to a Python engine.
- TWO viable off-the-shelf "import structured format -> edit visually -> export back -> editor owns NO file"
  editors: (1) bpmn-js (importXML/saveXML over standard BPMN 2.0 XML; XML stays externally owned; needs DI
  layout to render -> bpmn-auto-layout generates it deterministically). (2) draw.io/diagrams.net EMBED mode
  (iframe + postMessage: init -> load{xml} -> save/autosave{xml}; HOST owns storage; Apache-2.0; drawio-mcp
  exists). BOTH are JS/browser -> run as an OUT-OF-PROCESS edit-time tool (fine per I4).
- BECAUSE rebar won't persist the visual format: layout-in-git / clean-diff / merge-on-layout gotchas
  DISAPPEAR; what REMAINS = semantic round-trip fidelity, STABLE IDs across the round-trip, re-layout on
  every open (mitigated by deterministic auto-layout; or a git-ignored ephemeral layout sidecar).

RANKED CONCLUSION: (1) if semantics are BPMN-shaped -> IR<->BPMN 2.0 XML + bpmn-js; (2) general diagrams ->
IR<->draw.io mxGraphModel + draw.io embed; (3) AVOID MDE frameworks. Open Q (empirical, spike-worthy): does
rebar's IR map cleanly to BPMN (agent/LLM step has NO native BPMN concept -> serviceTask+extension; fan-out
-> multi-instance), or is draw.io's free-form mxGraphModel + a semantic-tagging convention the safer general
choice? Do the editors preserve UNKNOWN/extension attributes so agent-step metadata survives a round-trip?
Is auto-layout deterministic enough, or persist an ephemeral git-ignored layout sidecar?

=== CONVERGED ARCHITECTURE (synthesis of 4 research rounds + 2 spikes) ===
1. SOURCE OF TRUTH (git): rebar's own LLM-friendly DECLARATIVE workflow IR (v1 YAML -> v2) extended with
   DECLARATIVE control-flow as DATA (route/conditional-edges, loop frames, map/fan-out) + STABLE element IDs.
2. EXECUTION: extend the homegrown thin executor into a synchronous deterministic worklist INTERPRETER over
   the declarative IR (spike option a). LangGraph stays the per-agent-step runtime ONLY. Iteration-keyed
   run-state markers + mandatory max_iterations.
3. LLM EDITING (I2): agents edit the IR via ATOMIC ID-keyed ops (MCP/CLI/file) + validate-then-repair vs
   schema+linter (BPMN-Assistant pattern applied to OUR IR). Optional structured-output/grammar constraint.
4. HUMAN VISUAL EDIT (I1), EPHEMERAL: a THIN IR<->standard serializer projects the IR to a standard an
   off-the-shelf embeddable editor reads natively (bpmn-js+BPMN, or draw.io+mxGraphModel), edited in a
   transient view, round-tripped back; visual format NEVER committed. Editor = out-of-process edit-time tool.
5. ONLY BESPOKE PIECE: the thin ID/ordering/metadata-preserving IR<->standard serializer. Everything else
   off-the-shelf. NOVELTY CHECK: every component is a proven pattern (CWL/GHA/ASL declarative IR; thin
   interpreter; BPMN-Assistant atomic-ops; bpmn-js/draw.io ephemeral-projection) — no novel-no-prior-art part.

LAST MAJOR OPEN FORK: which editor/format for the ephemeral visual layer — bpmn-js+BPMN vs draw.io+
mxGraphModel — likely resolved by a small serializer+round-trip SPIKE on a real rebar workflow.
